scenarios.label: REFINED post-DESI 2025: m_1 = gamma^2 * m_3 = m_3/100
scenarios.verdict: WITHIN_DESI_2025_STRICT_AND_ABOVE_OSC_LOWER; NOTE: refined Sum 0.0591 eV is essentially the m_1->0 oscillation lower bound 0.0586 eV plus 0.5 meV; not distinguishable from m_1->0 limit at current DESI precision

verdict: FALSIFIABLE_NEUTRINO_TENSION: existing m_1 = m_3/(d+1) closure gives Sum = 0.0732 eV, sitting 1.7% over DESI 2024 bound of 0.072 eV. The alternative closure m_1 = m_3/N_gen^2 = m_3/9 gives Sum = 0.0658 eV (within DESI bound) and uses the cleaner generation-count primitive N_gen=3 instead of (d+1)=5. The current framework prediction is right at the experimental edge; DESI 2025+ tightening will either confirm m_1 = m_3/(d+1) or force the framework to adopt m_1 = m_3/N_gen^2.
REFINED_2026_05_10: Bundle updated to include the refined m_1 = gamma^2 * m_3 = m_3/100 closure used in P3 manuscript line 1370+ post-DESI 2025. Caveat: the refined Sum=0.0591 eV is only 0.5 meV above the oscillation lower bound; per peer review, the closure is empirically indistinguishable from m_1 -> 0 at current DESI precision.
